name: Push Notification Service
description: Design a push notification delivery system for mobile apps. Covers device token management, message queuing, platform-specific delivery (APNs/FCM), delivery tracking, and retry handling.

learnSteps.title: Notification Trigger and Queuing
learnSteps.description: When a backend event occurs (new message, order update, friend request), the App Server publishes a notification payload to Kafka with the target user ID, message content, and priority. Using a queue decouples notification delivery from business logic, ensuring the API response is not blocked by slow downstream delivery. Kafka partitions by user ID to maintain per-user ordering.

learnSteps.title: Device Token Resolution
learnSteps.description: Notification Workers consume from Kafka, look up the target user's device tokens in PostgreSQL (a user may have multiple devices), and determine the platform (iOS/Android) for each token. Stale tokens (devices where the user uninstalled the app) are detected when APNs/FCM returns an 'unregistered' error, and are pruned from the database.

learnSteps.title: Platform-Specific Delivery
learnSteps.description: Workers maintain persistent HTTP/2 connections to APNs and gRPC connections to FCM for efficient delivery. iOS notifications use APNs with certificate-based auth, while Android uses FCM with server keys. Workers batch multiple notifications per connection for throughput. Delivery receipts from each platform are recorded for analytics (delivered, opened, failed).

learnSteps.title: Retry, Deduplication, and Rate Limiting
learnSteps.description: Transient failures (network timeout, rate limiting) trigger exponential backoff retries. Permanent failures (invalid token) skip retries and update the DB. Per-user deduplication prevents duplicate notifications if the same event is processed twice. Rate limiting per user (e.g., max 10 notifications/hour) prevents notification fatigue and reduces churn.
